prompt: Produce a concise literature-style research report on graph neural networks (GNNs). Include: (1) what GNNs are and why graphs need specialized neural architectures, (2) core message-passing formulation, (3) seminal and widely-used architectures such as spectral GCN/ChebNet, GCN, GraphSAGE, GAT, GIN, MPNN, graph transformers, and equivariant GNNs, (4) key application domains, with special attention to molecular/protein/biomedical uses, (5) main limitations and failure modes such as over-smoothing, over-squashing, heterophily, scalability, data leakage, explainability, and OOD generalization, (6) current research directions as of 2026, and (7) a short bibliography of important papers with provenance/citations. Keep it grounded and avoid overclaiming.

ollama_response_raw.reasoning_summary: compound: literature search + report generation on GNNs, including architecture, applications, limitations, and future directions, with provenance tracking
